title: SSA Animals Methods Catalog
description: Fixed method definitions for animal husbandry, handling, welfare, breeding, feeding, health, housing, transport, and legally compliant slaughter/processing across commonly farmed and traditionally consumed species. Includes indigenous/culture-specific, natural, and modern methods. Designed for SSA session blueprints (steps + cues + checklists + timers).

safety.disclaimer: Always follow local laws, veterinary guidance, and humane handling/slaughter regulations. If you are inexperienced, work with a mentor, licensed processor, or veterinarian.

methods.title: Daily Welfare Rounds
methods.summary: A fast, consistent daily check across groups to catch problems early: water, feed, posture, breathing, manure, injuries, predators, and enclosure integrity.

appliesTo.notes: Adapt route order to reduce stress (quietest groups first).

steps.title: Start with quiet observation (2–5 minutes per area)
cues: Stand still; watch movement and breathing.
Listen for coughing, sneezing, abnormal vocalization.
checklist: Count animals visually vs expected headcount

steps.title: Water check + clean refill if needed
cues: Check flow/nipples; look for algae or slime.
checklist: Verify at least one functioning water point per group

steps.title: Feed + forage check
cues: Look for refusal, sorting, or sudden appetite change.
checklist: Confirm feeder access for smaller/weaker animals

steps.title: Health scan
cues: Check eyes/nose discharge, lameness, bloating, diarrhea, feather/coat condition.
checklist: Identify any animal needing isolate pen

steps.title: Fence/house integrity + predator signs
cues: Look for digging, tracks, bent wire, loose latches.
checklist: Inspect electric fence voltage if used

steps.title: Log findings + trigger tasks
cues: If any red flags, create a 'care session' in SSA immediately.

methods.title: Biosecurity Entry/Exit Protocol
methods.summary: Simple biosecurity routine to reduce disease introduction and spread between pens/pastures.

appliesTo.notes: Critical for poultry/swine; scale to operation size.

steps.title: Designate clean/dirty zones
checklist: Store clean PPE separate from dirty

steps.title: Entry routine
checklist: Put on pen-specific gloves if needed

steps.title: Tool control
cues: Never mix bleach with acids/ammonia; follow product labels.
checklist: Use dedicated tools per zone when possible

steps.title: Visitor policy
steps.notes: Consider a 48–72 hour livestock-free window for visitors when feasible.

methods.title: Rotational Grazing (Paddock System)
methods.summary: Move animals through paddocks to protect pasture, manage parasites, and improve forage utilization.

appliesTo.notes: Works with traditional herding patterns and modern polywire paddocks.

steps.title: Plan rotation map
cues: Rest length depends on season/growth rate.

steps.title: Move animals calmly
cues: Use pressure-and-release; avoid chasing.

methods.title: Fodder Sprouting System (Low-Tech)
methods.summary: Sprout grains/legumes to supplement greens when pasture is limited; common in many traditional systems and modern micro-farms.

appliesTo.notes: Follow safe sprouting hygiene; discard any moldy batches.

steps.title: Select seed and rinse
cues: Avoid cracked/moldy seed.

steps.title: Soak + drain
checklist: Drain fully to prevent standing water

steps.title: Rinse twice daily
cues: If sour smell or visible mold, discard.

steps.title: Harvest at target length
cues: Typically 5–7 days depending on temperature.

steps.title: Introduce gradually
checklist: Start small to avoid digestive upset
steps.notes: Fodder is a supplement; keep a fiber base for ruminants.

methods.title: Breeding Plan + Records (Seasonal)
methods.summary: A structured breeding plan: selection, pairing, timing, and records to improve outcomes and prevent inbreeding.

appliesTo.notes: Align breeding windows to forage availability and climate.

steps.title: Define breeding goal
cues: One primary goal reduces conflicting selections.
checklist: Meat, milk, fiber, eggs, work, conservation, show, dual-purpose

steps.title: Prevent inbreeding
cues: Use a simple sire rotation plan if no software.

steps.title: Set breeding window and exposure days
cues: Shorter windows produce tighter birthing windows.

methods.title: Heat Detection (Basic Signals)
methods.summary: Simple, species-appropriate heat detection for timely breeding. Includes low-tech observation and optional tools.

appliesTo.notes: Bird breeding is handled via flock management and nesting behaviors.

steps.title: Set observation times
cues: Heat signs are often brief.

steps.title: Look for primary signs
cues: Standing to be mounted, tail flagging, vocalization, swelling/discharge (species-dependent).

steps.title: Use teaser/marker if available
cues: Use safely and per welfare guidance.
checklist: Teaser male or marking harness (as appropriate)

steps.title: Confirm and schedule breeding
steps.notes: When uncertain, extend observation rather than rushing.

methods.title: Parasite Management with Fecal Monitoring
methods.summary: Targeted deworming and pasture management using fecal checks rather than routine blanket treatments.

appliesTo.notes: Coordinate with a veterinarian/lab; resistance is a serious risk.

steps.title: Set sampling cadence
cues: High-risk: young, lactating, thin animals.
checklist: Baseline: spring + late summer (or as advised)

steps.title: Collect samples hygienically
cues: Wear gloves; wash hands afterward.

steps.title: Treat selectively
cues: Follow label/legal use; withdrawal compliance is mandatory.

steps.title: Pasture + nutrition adjustments
steps.notes: Record changes and recheck to validate.

methods.title: Quarantine New Arrivals
methods.summary: Reduce disease introduction with a quarantine routine and observation period for new animals.

appliesTo.notes: Duration and tests vary by species and local disease risk.

steps.title: Arrival intake check
cues: Document seller info and transport time.

steps.title: Graduated introduction
steps.notes: Mixing too fast is a common cause of stress and injury.

methods.title: Deep Litter Bedding System
methods.summary: Manage bedding by layering dry carbon materials to control odor and moisture; used in many traditional barns and modern systems.

appliesTo.notes: Monitor ammonia and moisture; adjust ventilation.

steps.title: Daily spot management
cues: If ammonia smell is strong, bedding is too wet or ventilation is insufficient.

steps.title: Ventilation check
checklist: Open vents without drafts on animals
steps.notes: Balance fresh air with warmth, especially for young stock.

steps.title: Turn or cap layer
cues: Avoid creating dusty conditions.

steps.title: End-of-season cleanout + composting
cues: Wear mask if dusty; ammonia can irritate lungs.

methods.title: Low-Stress Handling (Pressure & Release)
methods.summary: Humane handling principles that reduce injury and improve productivity: point of balance, flight zone, calm movement.

appliesTo.notes: Adjust to species; avoid loud noises and rough handling.

steps.title: Use pressure-and-release
cues: Step into flight zone to start movement; step out to reward.

steps.title: Pause and reset if stress rises
cues: If bunching, backing, or vocalization increases, slow down.
checklist: Give animals time to see the path forward

methods.title: Humane Slaughter Planning (Compliance & Readiness)
methods.summary: Plan a legal, humane slaughter day: scheduling, transport, welfare prep, equipment readiness, and cold-chain.

appliesTo.notes: Local laws vary; many operations must use licensed facilities for certain species or sales channels.

steps.title: Confirm legal path
cues: When in doubt, use a licensed processor.

steps.title: Animal welfare prep
checklist: Follow appropriate fasting guidance per species/vet
steps.notes: Maintain water access unless directed otherwise.

steps.title: Sanitation plan
cues: Food safety is non-negotiable. Use potable water and approved sanitizers.

steps.title: Cold chain readiness
cues: Rapid chilling reduces spoilage risk.

methods.title: Cold Chain & Aging (Meat Quality)
methods.summary: Cold-chain and aging guidance for better tenderness and safety, adjusted by species and scale.

appliesTo.notes: Aging depends on species, fat cover, and cooler control. Follow food-safety guidance.

steps.title: Immediate chilling plan
checklist: Move product into chilling environment quickly

steps.title: Choose aging window (if applicable)
cues: Skip aging if you cannot control temperature/humidity.

steps.title: Record yield + quality notes
checklist: Note tenderness outcomes for next time

methods.title: Cut Sheet Planning (Whole Animal)
methods.summary: Create a cut plan that matches household preferences: roasts vs ground, organ meats, bones, fat, and preservation routing.

appliesTo.notes: This is planning/coordination; execution may be by a licensed processor.

steps.title: Preservation routing
checklist: Fresh vs frozen vs cured vs canned

steps.title: Import into SSA inventory
steps.notes: Link batch to animal ID and processing date.

methods.title: Deep Clean Poultry Coop
methods.summary: Periodic coop sanitation to reduce ammonia and parasites.

steps.title: Wash surfaces
cues: Avoid aerosolizing dust.

methods.title: Swine Wallow & Shade Management
methods.summary: Maintain cooling options for pigs in hot weather.

steps.title: Provide shade
cues: Heat stress rises quickly.

steps.title: Monitor for heat stress
cues: Open-mouth breathing, lethargy, piling in shade.

methods.title: Rabbit Heat Management
methods.summary: Prevent heat stress for rabbits with airflow, frozen bottles, and timing.

methods.title: Aquaculture Water Quality Basics
methods.summary: Routine checks for dissolved oxygen, temperature, and clarity; prevents losses.

steps.title: Measure key parameters
cues: Low oxygen is a common emergency.

steps.title: Record + respond
checklist: Trigger corrective task if out of range

methods.title: Camelid Fiber Care Routine
methods.summary: Routine grooming and fiber management for alpaca/llama/camelids.

methods.title: Mineral & Salt Program
methods.summary: Provide species-appropriate minerals/salt; monitor intake and adjust by season.

steps.title: Plan scope + gather supplies
cues: Bundle tasks in SSA for repeats.

steps.title: Monitor and adjust
cues: If unsure, consult a veterinarian/experienced handler.
checklist: Watch for stress or adverse outcomes
